name: Open House
subtitle: Central Embassy, 6th Floor
cost: Free (buy a coffee if you want)
description: Top floor of Central Embassy. Part bookstore, part open lounge. Massive space, nobody bothers you. The go-to free workspace for people who know.
tips: Weekday mornings are empty. Weekends get crowded with families. Grab a seat near the windows for natural light.

name: Paper Plane Project
subtitle: Thonglor Soi 17
description: Purpose-built for nomads. Library area, private call rooms, plugs on every desk, 8-hour wifi with any purchase. Turns into a bar with live bands Friday/weekends after 7pm. 95% laptop-friendly rating.
tips: One drink gets you 8 hours of wifi. The Crispy Pork Scrambled Eggs are legendary. Upload speed (66 Mbps) is better than download (15 Mbps) — great for video calls.

name: Starbucks Reserve
subtitle: ICONSIAM, Riverside
description: Two-story Starbucks Reserve with floor-to-ceiling river views. Huge space means you always find a seat. The view alone changes your energy.
tips: Go upstairs for quieter seating. Weekday mornings are dead — feels like your private office. Shuttle boat from Saphan Taksin is free.

name: TCDC
subtitle: Thailand Creative & Design Center, Charoenkrung
cost: Free (library area) / 100 THB day pass (resource center)
description: Government-funded design library. Quiet floors, fast wifi, surrounded by design and architecture books. The resource center has curated collections worth browsing between deep work sessions.
tips: The 5th floor co-working area has the best outlets. Closed Mondays. Bring your own charger cable — outlets are Thai-style.

name: Samyan CO-OP
subtitle: Samyan Mitrtown
best_hours: Open 24 hours (co-living zone on ground floor)
description: Mall library with a 24-hour zone on the ground floor. Air-conditioned, well-lit, plenty of outlets. The upstairs library is proper quiet. Downstairs co-living zone never closes. Multiple seating zones for solo or group work.
tips: The 24-hour zone gets packed with Chula students during exam season (Nov-Dec, Apr-May). Otherwise it's great. The mall food court is cheap.

name: Too Fast To Sleep
subtitle: Sam Yan
description: The original Bangkok late-night work cafe. 24 hours, cheap drinks, surrounded by Chula students grinding. Not quiet, not pretty — but it's open when everything else is closed.
tips: Don't come here for peace. Come here for energy at 2am when you need to push through. The iced chocolate is weirdly good.

name: The Commons
subtitle: Thonglor Soi 17
description: Open-air community mall in Thonglor. Multiple cafes and restaurants. The top floor (The Garden) has communal tables. More social than productive, but the energy is good for creative work.
tips: Power outlets are scarce — bring a full charge. The Roast cafe on the top floor is the most laptop-friendly tenant. Gets loud after 17:00.

name: Neilson Hays Library
subtitle: Surawong Road, Silom
description: Bangkok's oldest English-language library, built 1922. Colonial architecture, garden courtyard, genuinely peaceful. The cafe in the garden is where you work. Inside the library itself is members-only but the garden and cafe are open.
tips: Limited outlets so charge up first. The garden is magical in the morning. Membership is 1,500 THB/year if you want inside access.

name: Featherstone Bistro & Lifestyle
subtitle: Ekkamai Soi 12
description: Big airy cafe in Ekkamai with high ceilings and natural light. Good brunch menu. The back area is quieter and more work-friendly. Staff don't mind long stays.
tips: The back section past the bar has more outlets. Avoid the front patio if you need to focus — it faces the soi.

name: TrueSpace
subtitle: CentralWorld, 4th Floor
cost: Free (True customers) / Buy a True SIM
description: True (Thai telecom) runs free coworking spaces in malls. CentralWorld location is the biggest. Fast wifi (it's a telecom company), plenty of outlets, meeting rooms bookable. Just need a True SIM or account.
tips: If you don't have True, grab a tourist SIM for 300 THB — you get the workspace free forever. Also in Siam Paragon and other CentralPlaza malls.

name: KLOUD by KBANK
subtitle: Siam Square Soi 7
description: KBank's free coworking space in Siam Square. Multiple seating zones, meeting rooms for group work, vending machines for snacks. Free wifi and power outlets everywhere. One of the best-kept secrets in central Bangkok.
tips: Meeting rooms are first-come-first-served. The upper floor is quieter. Right in the middle of Siam — food options everywhere within walking distance.

name: BACC Art Library
subtitle: Bangkok Art & Culture Centre
description: Art library inside BACC. Quiet, air-conditioned, surrounded by art books and exhibitions. Work between gallery visits. The building itself is worth exploring on breaks.
tips: Closed Mondays. The cafe on the ground floor has decent coffee if you need a break. Free exhibitions change monthly — good for creative inspiration between work blocks.

name: Noble PLAY
subtitle: Ploenchit Road
description: Noble property developer's community space right at BTS Ploenchit. Beautiful interior, mix of sofa zones and work desks. Cafe on-site. Nobody checks if you're a Noble resident — just walk in.
tips: Closes at 5pm which limits it for late workers. But for a free morning-to-afternoon workspace next to BTS, it's hard to beat. Gets quiet after lunch.

name: Bank of Thailand Library
subtitle: Bang Khun Phrom, Riverside
description: Library inside the Bank of Thailand learning center with Chao Phraya river views. Quiet, well-maintained, free wifi and charging stations. One of the most peaceful workspaces in Bangkok that nobody talks about.
tips: The river-view seats are the prize. Come early to grab one. Open until 8pm — unusually late for a government library. Closed Mondays.

name: William Warren Library
subtitle: Jim Thompson Art Center
description: Library inside the Jim Thompson Art Center. Small but beautiful. Curated book collection on Thai art, architecture, and culture. Cafe on-site. Exhibition space doubles as a break spot.
tips: Only open Wed–Sun. Limited power outlets — bring a full charge. Best for short focused sessions (2-3 hours) with gallery breaks. The cafe makes good matcha.
